detailed_analysis: src/agents_sync/adoption/adopter.py is a Layer-2 use-case mixin (AdopterMixin) implementing the adopt-new-pair and one-way N-way sync flows for AdoptionEngine. The file is 294 lines (under the 300-line module limit) with one class and eight methods. It is structurally clean for a use-case orchestrator: it never references a concrete tool name (goal 5 / NFR-11 honoured), routes every destructive write through the archive gateway before write (I-3 / NFR-01 honoured via _archive_source_before_write and _archive_prior_slot_results), and delegates the secret-policy egress obligation (NFR-15) down to source_io.parse and render_to_agentic_tool rather than re-implementing it. The dominant smells are two long methods that each exceed the 40-line guideline and carry elevated cyclomatic complexity (_project_to_other_tools, _adopt_from_agentic_tool), and a near-duplication between _adopt_from_agentic_tool and _sync_from_agentic_tool (DRY). There is also untyped use of Any for source_io/source_info in _archive_source_before_write where the rest of the file uses precise types. No CRITICAL findings: no secrets, no injection, no silent swallowing (the one except is logged with structured context and returns a documented fallback), no mutable defaults, no no-op bodies. The mixin's reliance on undeclared attributes (self.config, self.agentic_tools, self.state_dir) and sibling methods is a mixin-coupling pattern that is documented in the class docstring but is structurally an inappropriate-intimacy/typing gap.

findings.problem: Method spans lines 172-233 (62 lines, ~50 non-blank code lines) over the 40-line guideline. Branch keywords (for + 3 guard ifs with compound conditions + 2 inline conditionals on target_info) push cyclomatic complexity to ~11, above the >10 threshold. It mixes result-seeding, per-tool prior-text read, two skip gates, a reserved-name gate with logging, and the render call.

findings.evidence:
def _adopt_from_agentic_tool(self, pair_id, source_tool, info, state) -> bool:
    source_info = info.agentic_tools[source_tool]
    source_spec = self.agentic_tools[source_tool]
    ...
    text = read_artifact_text(...)
    canonical = source_io.parse(...)
    if self._skip_private_canonical(...): return False
    canonical["pair_id"] = pair_id
    if not source_info.pair_id_present: ... write_artifact_inplace(...)
    save_canonical(...)
    results = self._project_to_other_tools(...)
    self._archive_prior_slot_results(...)
    update_state_n_way(...)
    logging.info(...)
findings.problem: Method spans lines 52-105 (54 lines). It performs source read+parse, privacy gate, pair_id injection, conditional archive+inplace-rewrite, canonical save, projection, prior-slot archive, state update, and logging — multiple sequential responsibilities in one body. Exceeds the 40-line guideline.

findings.evidence:
Lines 141-145 (sync) duplicate 61-65 (adopt):
    source_info = info.agentic_tools[source_tool]
    source_spec = self.agentic_tools[source_tool]
    source_io = source_spec.io[info.kind]
    source_root = expand_path(self.config[source_spec.config_dir_keys[info.kind]])
    text = read_artifact_text(source_io, source_info.path, slot=source_info.slot)
and lines 154-166 duplicate 86,97-99 (save_canonical, source_dir computation, _project_to_other_tools, _archive_prior_slot_results, update_state_n_way).
findings.problem: _sync_from_agentic_tool and _adopt_from_agentic_tool share the source-resolution preamble (source_info/source_spec/source_io/source_root/text), the parse+privacy-gate sequence, the source_dir computation, and the project+archive+state-update tail almost verbatim. The only material differences are prior_canonical (None vs load_canonical), the conditional inplace rewrite, and read_prior_text (False vs True). The shared body is a Rule-of-Three candidate for extraction.

source_io and source_info are typed Any while the rest of the file uses precise types (AgenticToolSpec, AgenticToolInfo, CustomizationTypeIO would be the natural choices, as in _read_target_prior_text at lines 240-241). The method reads source_io.file_layout and source_info.slot/.path, so Any disables type checking at exactly the archive boundary that backs NFR-01. Weakens the name contract per the project's public-interface annotation rule.

findings.variant: Inappropriate intimacy via undeclared mixin attributes
findings.evidence:
class AdopterMixin:
    """... Relies on ``self.config``, ``self.agentic_tools``, ``self.state_dir``, and methods from :class:`AdoptionEngine` (``_pick_winner``, ``_available_participating_tools``, etc.)."""
    ...
    self._skip_private_canonical(...)  # line 72
    self._target_is_private(...)        # line 202
    self._is_reserved_target_name(...)  # line 212
    self._available_participating_tools(info.kind)  # line 189
findings.problem: The mixin reads self.config, self.agentic_tools, self.state_dir and calls _skip_private_canonical, _target_is_private, _is_reserved_target_name, _available_participating_tools without declaring any of them; they exist only on the composed AdoptionEngine and sibling mixins. This is the documented mixin pattern (the docstring names the dependencies), but it is structurally untypable coupling: a type checker cannot verify these attributes/methods exist, and a reader cannot find the contract without leaving the file. Flagged WARNING, not MAJOR, because the docstring partially mitigates and the coupling is intra-class-cluster.

findings.evidence:
archive.archive_text(
    self.state_dir, pair_id, tool,
    slot_name=str(result.slot),
    extension=extension or ".json",
    content=result.prior_slot_text,
)
findings.problem: The fallback file extension ".json" is an inline literal with no named constant or comment explaining why json is the default archive suffix when both file_layout.file_suffix and path.suffix are empty. Minor; intent is recoverable but not self-documenting per the no-hardcoding guideline.

verdict_summary: Clean, tool-agnostic, archive-before-write use-case mixin; two over-length methods and a DRY duplication between adopt/sync cap structure at 0.5 (MAJOR). No CRITICAL findings and full spec/architecture conformance.
